description_long: This object is a realistic model of a crab, featuring a broad, slightly flattened carapace with a mottled brown and tan coloration. The crab has two prominent claws, with the right claw being significantly larger and more robust than the left, a characteristic often seen in fiddler crabs. Its eight walking legs are slender and jointed, splaying outward from the body and ending in pointed tips. The carapace displays subtle textural details, including small bumps and natural patterning, while the claws have a slightly glossy, chitinous appearance. The eyes are positioned on short stalks at the front of the carapace, and no antennae are clearly visible. The overall form and coloration suggest a species adapted for camouflage in sandy or rocky urban environments.
description: A lifelike crab with a broad, mottled carapace, one oversized claw, and eight slender legs, featuring detailed textures and natural brown coloration.
description_view_0: Front view showing the crab's wide carapace, both claws, and forward-facing eyes on short stalks.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the large right claw and the segmentation of the walking legs.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the full spread of the eight legs and the dorsal patterning on the carapace.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view emphasizing the smaller left claw and the curvature of the body.
